title: Vol-estimate floor in vol_target_scale (bound k against vol-collapse)
source: risk-officer review of B0108: 'required regardless of any cap decision'. Split out as the safe, ungated sliver of B0108.
why: vol_target_scale computes k = target_vol / book_vol clamped only by max_leverage. As the ex-ante book vol estimate collapses toward 0 (a near-flat price window), k blows up, bounded ONLY by the leverage cap. This is the classic lever-into-calm-then-spike failure mode of vol targeting. A vol-estimate floor on the denominator bounds k by construction, independent of any cap decision. At the current cap 0.5 a low default floor never bites (book_vol ~4.6% >> floor), so behavior is UNCHANGED today; the floor is the prerequisite safety rail for any future cap raise (deferred B0108).
value_rationale: M: safety hardening of a live-book sizing primitive; no exposure change today, closes a real tail hole.
scope: Add a min_book_vol floor parameter to vol_target_scale; k = target_vol / max(book_vol, min_book_vol).
Default floor low enough to NOT change behavior at the current cap (regression-pinned).
Unit test: k is bounded when book_vol -> 0; behavior unchanged when book_vol is normal.
recommendation: Safe, decision-free, ships independently of B0108/B0112. Do now.

history.reason: split from B0108 per risk-officer: the safe ungated sliver
vol-estimate floor implemented (TDD); default 0.5% inert at current cap, backtest headline unchanged, parity green
